channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: SA Polls Suggest Labor Landslide as One Nation Surpasses Liberals
item.description: The South Australian state election is set for Saturday, with polling indicating a strong performance for Labor. Recent surveys from Newspoll, DemosAU, YouGov, and Fox & Hedgehog suggest that Labor’s ...

item.title: Government must fully disclose SGR extension contract details and costs
item.description: The editorial urges the Kenyan government to operate with maximum transparency in the upcoming Standard Gauge Railway (SGR) extension project, warning that past failures of openness have bred public s...

item.title: Trump pushes Australia to increase involvement in Middle East conflict
item.description: US President Donald Trump has publicly urged the Albanese government to take a greater role in the escalating Middle East conflict, amid mounting global tension and energy market disruption. Frustrate...

item.title: Mercy from Machakos Battles Kidney Disease After Losing Two Children, Seeks Support for Transplant
item.description: Mercy, a young woman from Mlolongo, Machakos, has been through intense personal hardship. In 2022, she moved in with her boyfriend and got pregnant, but tragically lost her first baby due to complicat...

item.title: U.S. Sanctions Waiver Allows Iranian Oil Sales at Sea but Blocks Payments
item.description: The U.S. Treasury announced a limited sanctions waiver on Friday, March 20, 2026, permitting the sale, transportation, and unloading of Iranian oil already loaded onto ships before that date. This mea...

item.title: UK temperatures to fall sharply after recent warm spell, with showers and potential snow in Scotland
item.description: Following the UK’s warmest day of the year so far, the Met Office has issued a forecast for a significant drop in temperatures across the country. On Wednesday, some areas saw temperatures peak at 20C...

item.title: Sindh Government’s Wedding Guest Limit Causes Public Discontent and Industry Concerns
item.description: The Sindh government has announced a restriction limiting the number of guests at wedding and other social functions to 200 people, as part of its austerity measures following the Middle East conflict...

item.title: How India’s response to the Iran war is testing its claim to strategic autonomy
item.description: The article explains how India’s handling of the latest U.S.–Israel war on Iran has sparked a serious debate about the country’s foreign policy. The author, a former Indian diplomat, notes that some p...

item.title: NCoS, Ministry Partner to Support Inmate Rehabilitation and Poverty Reduction
item.description: The Nigerian Correctional Service (NCoS) and the Federal Ministry of Humanitarian Affairs and Poverty Reduction don dey collaborate serious, to do better for inmates and make sure dem fit reintegrate ...

item.title: United States issues temporary waiver to allow global sale of Iranian oil amid ongoing conflict
item.description: The United States government has granted a temporary 30-day sanctions waiver allowing the sale of Iranian crude oil and petroleum products loaded between March 20 and April 19, 2026. The waiver aims t...

item.title: Bafana Coach Hugo Broos to deploy Relebohile Mofokeng as central playmaker
item.description: Bafana Bafana coach Hugo Broos has announced that Orlando Pirates attacker Relebohile Mofokeng will be given the chance to play as a number 10 playmaker in upcoming friendly matches against Panama. Th...

item.title: Goldberg to stage Golden Fiesta in Benin featuring Shallipopi and other Afro-pop stars
item.description: Goldberg, the award-winning Nigerian premium lager, don dey prepare for one big celebration wey dem call Golden Fiesta. The event go happen for Garrick Memorial, Benin, on Friday, April 3, 2026. The f...

item.title: Alberta police investigate highway shooting that killed Sikh international student Birinder Singh
item.description: Birinder Singh, a 22-year-old international student from Punjab, India, was tragically killed after being shot by someone in a passing pickup truck while driving on Highway 2 near Leduc, Alberta. Sing...

item.title: Iran-Israel Strikes Disrupt Key Oil and Gas Infrastructure in Persian Gulf
item.description: The ongoing war between Israel and Iran has escalated dramatically, with recent strikes targeting critical oil and gas facilities in the Persian Gulf region. Israel hit Iran's South Pars gas field, th...

item.title: Canadian mother and daughter from B.C. detained in Texas despite legal documentation
item.description: A British Columbia mother, Tania Warner, and her seven-year-old daughter Ayla, who have been living in Texas for five years, were detained by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) while retur...

item.title: Shujaa Eyes Top Four Finish in Division 2 to Qualify for Championships
item.description: The Kenya men's rugby sevens team, Shujaa, are gunning for a top-four finish in the ongoing World Series Division 2 tournament as they aim to secure promotion back to Division 1. Head coach Kevin 'Bli...

item.title: Alberta Law Society Ends Mandatory Indigenous Training Following New Provincial Law
item.description: The Law Society of Alberta has announced it will no longer require lawyers to complete Indigenous cultural competency training ahead of the implementation of the so-called 'Peterson law'. This decisio...

item.title: Balochistan police apprehend nearly 500 proclaimed offenders during 40-day operation
item.description: The Balochistan Police have successfully rounded up 497 proclaimed offenders in a dedicated 40-day operation targeting individuals wanted for serious crimes such as murder, attempted murder, robbery, ...

item.title: Tanzania Ports Authority Strengthens Marketing Efforts in Zimbabwe
item.description: Tanzania Ports Authority (TPA) imeonyesha maendeleo mazuri katika kuboresha huduma zake za bandari kupitia ofisi yake ya Harare, Zimbabwe. Hii ni sehemu ya utekelezaji wa Sera ya Diplomasia ya Kiuchum...

item.title: Justin Timberlake Pleads Guilty to Impaired Driving in Hamptons Incident
item.description: Police in Sag Harbor, New York, have released body camera footage showing Justin Timberlake's arrest for impaired driving in June 2024. The footage reveals the singer struggling to perform standard fi...
